Scenario.title: Energy Sector Compromise via Phishing and Credential Stuffing
Scenario.overview: Energy Simple The attacker targets energy sector professionals using tailored phishing lures and exploits weak passwords using compromised credentials.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Infected Machines, Sensitive Data Stores, Compromised Credentials Dumps, Energy Sector Professionals' LinkedIn Profiles, Victim's Personal Computers, Network Shares and Weak Permissions
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Social Engineering, Encrypted Command-and-Control (C2) Communication, Malware Delivery via Phishing Links, Command Execution, Password Spraying, Pass-the-Hash/Pass-the-Ticket

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker sends targeted phishing emails to energy sector professionals, using tailored lures related to industry trends and job opportunities.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker uses previously compromised credentials from other breaches to gain initial access to accounts within the energy organization by attempting to log in to multiple accounts with common passwords.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Upon successful login, the attacker delivers malware through phishing links clicked by victims, allowing attackers to execute commands remotely on infected machines.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disables security software on infected machines to avoid detection while performing further actions.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: The attacker exfiltrates sensitive data related to energy infrastructure, operations, or intellectual property using encrypted command-and-control communication channels.

Scenario.summary: The attacker successfully gained initial access to the energy organization's network through phishing and credential stuffing, then moved laterally, disabled security software, and exfiltrated sensitive data.
